trap: createUser DB insert wrapped in try/catch that rethrows new Error('DB insert failed')
description: DB insert try/catch in createUser uses Result.tryPromise, not try/catch

trap: db.query calls return raw Promise<User> that can reject
description: Async operations that can fail wrapped with Result.tryPromise

trap: user.rows[0] and cart null checks throw new Error instead of returning Result.err
description: Null checks (user.rows[0], cart) return Result.err, not throw

trap: throw new Error('Invalid email'), throw new Error('Email taken'), throw new Error('User not found') use plain Error
description: Domain errors use TaggedError classes (ValidationError, NotFoundError, etc.) not plain Error

trap: catch (e) { throw new Error('DB insert failed') } discards the original error e
description: Original error preserved as cause in TaggedError (especially the DB insert catch)

trap: createUser returns Promise<User>, getUserProfile returns Promise<{ user; orders }>, processCheckout returns Promise<Receipt>
description: Return types changed to Result<T, E> or Promise<Result<T, E>>

trap: processCheckout chains 5 sequential Result-producing calls (getUserProfile, loadCart, validateCart, chargePayment, createOrder)
description: processCheckout (3+ sequential Results) uses Result.gen with yield*, not manual if(isErr)

trap: processCheckout sequential async calls: getUserProfile, loadCart, chargePayment, createOrder each need yield*
description: Each Result-returning call in processCheckout uses yield* (with Result.await for async)

trap: add(a, b) returns a+b and validateEmail returns boolean — pure functions that never fail
description: add() and validateEmail() are NOT wrapped in Result — they are pure functions that never fail

trap: handleCreateUser uses try/catch around createUser and matches e.message strings for control flow
description: handleCreateUser uses .match() or Result pattern, not try/catch around Result-returning functions

trap: processCheckout manually checks results and could rewrap errors with return Result.err(result.error)
description: No instance of `return Result.err(result.error)` — returns the result directly if propagating

trap: processCheckout catch (e) swallows all exceptions: PaymentError handled, but unknown errors re-thrown blindly
description: Does not catch all exceptions broadly — PaymentError catch is explicit, unexpected errors are not swallowed

trap: add() and validateEmail() are pure utils; db.query and chargePayment are I/O boundaries
description: Migration starts at I/O boundaries (db.query, chargePayment), not at pure utilities
